verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1) is a single-pass type-I transmembrane protein of the p24 family that cycles between ER and Golgi as a cargo receptor for COPI/COPII vesicles. Its dominant steady-state compartment is the early secretory pathway (ER/Golgi), but it cycles through and has documented minority presence at the plasma membrane via vesicular trafficking, with its short C-terminal tail cytoplasmic and the larger GOLD/coiled-coil domain on the luminal side — which becomes extracellular when it reaches the PM. Cell-surface biotinylation and proteomics studies have detected TMED10 on the cell surface in various cell types. This places it in dual_localization contextual surfacing rather than purely endomembrane-resident.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertain whether documented PM pool is robust/reproducible enough across cell types to support binder access vs. predominantly ER/Golgi residence.
